input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-03T01:16:09.380Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** ÔÇö use `AppThemeBinding` to declare light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

It reacts to the OS theme automatically ÔÇö no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** ÔÇö use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
var page = this;

// SetAppThemeColor is a shorthand for Color properties
page.SetAppThemeColor(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,    // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(
    Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For non-color properties, use the generic SetAppTheme<T>
var icon = new Image();
icon.SetAppTheme<string>(
    Image.SourceProperty, "icon_light.png", "icon_dark.png");
```

---

**Key points:**

| | XAML | C# |
|---|---|---|
| Syntax | `{AppThemeBinding Light=ÔÇª, Dark=ÔÇª}` | `SetAppThemeColor()` / `SetAppTheme<T>()` |
| Works on | Any bindable property | Any bindable property |
| Auto-updates | Ô£à on theme change | Ô£à on theme change |

- Both approaches listen to `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and update live.
- You can also define theme-aware colors in `App.xaml` resources and reference them by key for better reuse.
- To **force** a theme regardless of system setting: `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;`
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to make a ContentPage theme-aware. This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code change in an existing repo. I'll provide a concise, practical answer with XAML and C# examples.
